story: Dolly Jameson, fifty-two, lived by a strict 6:15 a.m. routine. One Tuesday her alarm failed; she missed her bus, was baptized by filthy rain, and at work learned she was first up to present. The next mornings folded on themselves: ZoZo the cat spoke; the rain and five splashes repeated; the mirror told her, “Then close your eyes,” and each time she woke again at 6:15 as the clock flickered. Mr. Banda called: shareholders were waiting. The bus kept roaring past, spraying glass. Overnight, the transit authority taped a notice at the end of her street moving her usual bus stop two blocks east because of flooding. The morning her alarm returned to its faithful 6:15, rain began to fall inside her room. “You’re late, Dolly,” ZoZo said.
implausible_knowledge: Overnight, the transit authority taped a notice at the end of her street moving her usual bus stop two blocks east because of flooding.
question: When ZoZo tells her she’s late and she rushes to leave for work, what will Dolly most likely plan to catch the bus?
answer_with_knowledge: Head two blocks east to the temporary stop to catch the rerouted bus.
answer_without_knowledge: Hurry to her usual bus stop and try to catch the bus there.
